conjecture_title: Optimality of the $o(n^{3/2})$ bound in Corollary 1.4
paper_title: The regularity method for graphs with few 4-cycles
summary: No follow-up work settling the question of whether the exponent $3/2$ in Corollary 1.4 is best possible was found in the literature. Corollary 1.4 of arXiv:2004.10180 asserts that every $n$-vertex $C_5$-free graph can be made triangle-free by deleting $o(n^{3/2})$ edges; the tightness of this exponent remains an open question as of 2026. Related work (PCMI 2025 lecture notes by Wigderson) discusses $C_5$-free graph structure and edge-deletion bounds for bipartiteness but does not directly address the triangle-free exponent.
search_queries: C5-free triangle-free edge deletion n^{3/2} Conlon Fox Sudakov Zhao 2004.10180
C5-free graph triangle-free deletion exponent 3/2 optimality regularity method
arxiv "2004.10180" C5-free triangle-free tight exponent cite 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up paper found that proves or disproves the optimality of the $3/2$ exponent. Searches covered the paper's arXiv ID, the authors' names, and the key mathematical terms; the conjecture appears to remain open. The PCMI 2025 lecture notes (Wigderson) mention $C_5$-free graphs and edge-deletion bounds but target bipartiteness rather than triangle-freeness and do not resolve this specific question.
